semantic_journey: Arabic zajil relates to carrier pigeons or pigeon handlers, not a concrete implement. Greek zeugla = the yoke-loop or neckband of a yoke. Carrier pigeons and agricultural yoke-loops are entirely different semantic fields. The original reasoning appears to misidentify the Arabic root meaning.
key_insight: Arabic carrier pigeon (zajil) has no connection to Greek yoke-loop (zeugla) - different semantic fields, root misidentified.

semantic_journey: Arabic jamal = camel. Greek Gamla = city in the Golan Heights (from Aramaic/Hebrew gamal = camel, named for its camel-hump shaped hill). While the city name Gamla may derive from the camel concept, this is a proper place name - the city of Gamla - not a lexical cognate. City names derived from camel imagery are not discoveries.
key_insight: Greek Gamla is a city name possibly from Aramaic camel-shaped hill - a toponym, not a lexical root cognate.

semantic_journey: Arabic al-fathij includes meanings of naqasa (deficiency/shortage) and taraka (leaving behind/abandoning). Greek leipho = to leave, to lack, to be absent. The Arabic root captures leaving/lacking and Greek preserves exactly this semantic core: to leave = to be absent = to lack. Direct one-step semantic preservation of the leaving/lacking concept.
key_insight: Arabic leaving/lacking (al-fathij) aligns with Greek to-leave/lack (leibho) - direct preservation of deficiency concept.

semantic_journey: Same Arabic root al-fathij as GRC_0034. Greek is the perfect passive form meaning has been left/left behind. The semantic alignment is the same: Arabic lacking/leaving preserved in the Greek perfect form of the same verb. Same finding as GRC_0034.
key_insight: Same as GRC_0034 but Greek perfect form - Arabic leaving/lacking concept in Greek perfect passive.

semantic_journey: Arabic balsam = balsam resin. Greek opo-balsamon = juice/sap of the balsam tree (opo = juice). The same Semitic balsam term (GRC_0016) appears as a component in a Greek compound meaning balsam juice. The Arabic root b-l-s-m entered Greek directly and was used to form the compound for the liquid form of the resin. Confirmed material culture cognate (same as GRC_0016 in compound form).
key_insight: Balsam resin cognate appearing in Greek compound opo-balsamon (balsam juice) - confirmed material culture transmission.

semantic_journey: Arabic naft = naphtha, petroleum. Greek naftha = naphtha. Same as GRC_0026 but using the Arabic root naft. The Semitic root n-f-t for the flammable earth-liquid was transmitted to Greek. Material culture cognate confirmed.
key_insight: Arabic naft (naphtha) = Greek naftha - same Semitic naphtha root as GRC_0026, confirmed material culture cognate.

semantic_journey: Arabic and Greek are proper place names. Gigonos was a town in Macedonia; Arabic form is a different toponym. Proper names from different regions with no documented etymological link. Excluded as proper names.
key_insight: Two proper place names (Macedonian town vs Arabic toponym) - excluded as proper names.

semantic_journey: Arabic al-asr = pressing/squeezing (the action of extraction). Greek lasaron = laser, the juice of silphium (a specific plant extract from Cyrene). The chain pressing -> extracted liquid -> specifically silphium juice requires 3 steps with the specific plant identification being an unattested inferential leap. Lasaron names a specific resin not generic pressed juice. Too many steps.
key_insight: Arabic pressing/squeezing does not connect to specific Greek plant-extract name lasaron - too many inferential steps.
